displayTitle: The Booth Rules That Are Never Optional

beats.narration: Magnetic particle equipment runs at low voltage but thousands of amperes — and the booth holds hazards beyond the current. None are optional.

payload.title: BOOTH SAFETY
payload.sub: Thousands of amperes — and the booth's other hazards.

beats.narration: Break a live connection at a head, clamp, or prod and you draw an arc — burning flesh, leaving arc strikes that condemn parts.

payload.heading: Break a live contact, draw an arc

beats.narration: Ultraviolet lamps demand respect. Never stare into the lens, never run a cracked filter — and skin exposure adds up across a shift.

payload.heading: UV-A: view the surface, not the bulb
payload.note: NEVER A CRACKED FILTER · EXPOSURE ADDS UP

beats.narration: The consumables bite too: airborne powder is dust, oil mist causes dermatitis, petroleum carriers burn. The safety data sheet governs every product.

chips.s: No flames, no hot parts in oil

beats.narration: So the booth rule that is never optional: current off before any contact is broken. Every head, every clamp, every prod, every time.

payload.sub: Every head, clamp, and prod — every time.
